subUnits.title: 1.1 Immediate Family Vocabulary & Gender Rules
notes: In French, every noun has a gender: Masculine (le/un) or Feminine (la/une).
- Always learn the article (le/la) with the noun.
- 'la femme' means 'the wife' in marriage context, but 'the woman' generally.
- Nigerian families are often large; 'les parents' can refer to both parents and extended relatives in local context, but in French, it strictly means mother and father.
diagramDescriptions: Chart of immediate family with masculine/feminine pairs
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French immediate family vocabulary le pere la mere site:youtube.com

quiz.q: How do you say 'father' in French?
What is the feminine form of 'le fils'?
Which word means 'husband'?
What does 'la grand-mère' mean?
Which article is used for 'sœur'?
How do you say 'parents' in French?
What does 'le bébé' mean?
Which word means 'brother'?
In the context of marriage, 'la femme' means:
quiz.explanation: In marriage context, 'la femme' means wife; otherwise, it means woman.

quiz.q: What is the masculine form of 'la mère'?
quiz.explanation: The masculine form of 'mother' (la mère) is 'father' (le père).

subUnits.title: 1.2 Extended Family Vocabulary & Plural Forms
notes: - le cousin (cousin m.) / la cousine (cousin f.)
- Compound: both parts pluralized (le grand-père -> les grands-pères).
- Do not say 'un sœur'. Always match the article to the noun's gender: 'une sœur'.
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French extended family vocabulary plural rules site:youtube.com

quiz.q: How do you say 'uncle' in French?
What is the plural of 'le neveu'?
quiz.explanation: Words ending in -eau take -x in plural: les neveux.

quiz.q: Which word means 'aunt'?
What does 'la belle-mère' mean?
How do you say 'godfather'?
What is the plural of 'le grand-père'?
quiz.explanation: In compound nouns, both parts are pluralized: les grands-pères.

quiz.q: Which word means 'niece'?
What is the feminine form of 'le cousin'?
How do you say 'nephew'?
Which of these is an irregular plural?
quiz.explanation: 'les neveux' is irregular because 'neveu' ends in -eau, changing to -x.

subUnits.title: 1.3 Describing Your Family in French
notes: - Je suis enfant unique. (I am an only child.)
- Nous sommes [number] dans ma famille. (We are [number] in my family.)
- J'ai deux frères et une sœur. (I have two brothers and one sister.)
- Mon père s'appelle Emeka. (My father's name is Emeka.)
- Ma mère est enseignante. (My mother is a teacher.)
- Use these phrases to introduce your family members to a French speaker, incorporating Nigerian names like Chidi, Ngozi, or Amina.
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French describing family jai il sappelle site:youtube.com

quiz.q: How do you say 'I have two brothers'?
quiz.explanation: 'I have' is 'J'ai', and 'two brothers' is 'deux frères'.

quiz.q: What does 'Il s'appelle Emeka' mean?
quiz.explanation: 'Il s'appelle' means 'His name is' or 'He is called'.

quiz.q: How do you say 'I am an only child'?
quiz.explanation: 'I am an only child' is 'Je suis enfant unique'.

quiz.q: Which phrase means 'Her name is...'?
How do you say 'We are six in the family'?
opts: Nous sommes six dans la famille
quiz.explanation: 'We are six in the family' translates to 'Nous sommes six dans la famille'.

quiz.q: What is the correct translation of 'My mother is a teacher'?
How do you say 'I have one sister'?
Which sentence is grammatically correct?
opts: J'ai un frère et un sœur
J'ai un frère et une sœur
J'ai une frère et une sœur
J'ai un frère et une frères

quiz.q: What does 'Mes parents habitent à Lagos' mean?
How do you introduce your grandfather's name?
quiz.explanation: Grandfather is masculine, so use 'Il s'appelle...'.
